skill.name: senior-backend
skill.description: Backend development skill for designing APIs, reviewing backend code, planning database improvements, and using simple Python helper scripts for local project analysis reports.
skill.summary: Design and review backend APIs, databases, security patterns, and performance workflows.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported critical combinations, weak cryptography, network access, command execution, and environment file access. Context review found these are mostly false positives from markdown code fences, generated analysis text, and documentation; the real remaining concern is optional local JSON report writing to user-selected paths.

low_findings.title: User-selected report file writes
low_findings.description: The helper scripts write JSON output to the path supplied with the output option. This is legitimate local tooling behavior, but users should avoid sensitive paths because the file is opened for writing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly write output to a caller-provided path after JSON mode is enabled. The behavior is intentional and local, so the risk is low rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Command execution alerts are markdown false positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are markdown code fences and command examples in documentation. No Ruby backtick execution, subprocess call, eval call, or dynamic shell execution was found in the cited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation code fences or ordinary setup examples. A targeted text search found no real shell execution APIs in the skill scripts.

low_findings.title: Network, environment, and crypto alerts are unsupported by context
low_findings.description: The reported network finding appears in generated FAQ text, the environment finding is a documented development setup command, and weak crypto alerts point to descriptions or headings rather than cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose, setup documentation, or argparse descriptions. No requests library use, credential read, or weak hash implementation was found in the reviewed skill files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Writable output path accepted from CLI argument
dangerous_patterns.description: Each Python helper can write a JSON report to the user-supplied output path. This is a standard feature, but it can overwrite files if the user provides an unsafe destination.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is confirmed in all three scripts. It is constrained to local report output and does not show exfiltration or privilege escalation.

content.user_title: Build Backend APIs With Senior Review
content.value_statement: Backend projects often need consistent API, database, security, and performance decisions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured backend guidance with lightweight local helper scripts.
actual_capabilities: Guides REST and GraphQL API design decisions for backend services.
Provides reference notes for backend security, database optimization, and API design patterns.
Runs local Python helper scripts that validate a target path and produce basic analysis reports.
Exports helper script results as console output or optional JSON files.
Supports backend planning across Node.js, Express, Python, Go, PostgreSQL, and cloud deployment topics.
limitations: The helper scripts contain placeholder analysis logic, not complete production generators.
The load tester does not perform real network load testing in the reviewed code.
Database migration guidance still requires human review before use in production.
Security guidance is general and does not replace a project-specific security audit.

use_cases.title: Plan a New API Service
use_cases.description: Use the skill to outline endpoints, data models, validation needs, and implementation steps before writing service code.

use_cases.title: Review Backend Architecture
use_cases.description: Ask for a structured review of API boundaries, database access patterns, authentication flow, and operational risks.

use_cases.title: Prepare Performance Work
use_cases.description: Use the references and local scripts to organize performance checks, report results, and define follow-up improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Describe an API Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new service
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-backend skill to plan a REST API for a small task management service. Include endpoints, data models, validation rules, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Backend Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-backend skill to review this backend module for API design, error handling, database access, and security concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Database Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-backend database guidance to analyze slow PostgreSQL queries and propose indexing, pagination, caching, and migration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Backend
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-backend skill to design a production-ready GraphQL backend with authentication, authorization, observability, rate limits, and deployment checks.

output_examples.input: Plan a REST API for an order management service.
output: Endpoint groups for orders, customers, inventory, and payments.
Data model notes with key relationships and validation rules.
Security recommendations for authorization and rate limiting.
Testing checklist for unit, integration, and contract tests.

output_examples.input: Review a backend service that has slow list endpoints.
output: Likely causes such as missing pagination and repeated database calls.
Recommended indexes and query shape changes to validate in staging.
Caching and observability steps for measuring improvement.

output_examples.input: Create a security checklist for a new authentication flow.
output: Input validation and error handling checks.
Password, token, and session handling requirements.
Authorization tests for privileged and unprivileged users.
Deployment checks for secrets, logs, and monitoring.

best_practices: Run helper scripts only on local project paths that you intend to analyze.
Review generated reports and recommendations before changing production systems.
Pair the skill guidance with project tests, observability data, and security review.
anti_patterns: Do not treat placeholder script output as a complete production assessment.
Do not write reports to sensitive paths or shared system locations.
Do not apply database or security recommendations without reviewing project context.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan, review, and improve backend APIs, database access, security patterns, and performance workflows.

faq.question: Does it include executable tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes three Python helper scripts that validate a target path and can print or save basic reports.

faq.question: Does the load tester send network traffic?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the reviewed code. The script performs local placeholder analysis and report output.

faq.question: Can it replace a senior engineer review?
faq.answer: No. It provides structure and checks, but a qualified engineer should review production decisions.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users watch for?
faq.answer: Users should choose safe output paths and verify all recommendations against their actual codebase.
